feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Integration Suite
feed.subtitle: SAP Integration Suite Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Push data from a CSV file to SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) using Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I would like to know if it’s possible to push data from a CSV file into SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) using SAP Integration Suite.

 My goal:
Automate the data load process from an external source (CSV format) into SAC, ideally into a planning model or a dataset.

 What I’ve done so far:

  • CSV file is available and accessible via SFTP.

  • I’ve set up a basic iFlow in Integration Suite to read the file.

  • I’m now trying to understand how to push that data into SAC—what endpoint or API to use? What format is expected by SAC?

  • Any sample iFlow, blog post, or documentation to guide the process?

Thanks in advance for your help!

entry.title: SAP CPI: Not able to pick done file
entry.content:
Using Sender SFTP, I am trying to pick the Main file based on Read lock strategy as Completion file.

 

Main file Naming Format: abc.TXT
Done/completion File Format: abc_CMPL.TXT

What should be the format of Done file Naming. Seems it only supports when extension is .done.

Please suggest

entry.title: Certficate expiry notification in SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have received notification regarding the certificate expiry for Baltimore CyberTrust Root Certificate on 13th May 2025 in Integration Suite. We are not sure whether it really affects us,  There are certain steps that needs to be taken mentioned in https://me.sap.com/notes/3565626/E  . Eventhough this certificate is not used currently, it is better to update the certificate right? Pls guide regarding this.

 

 

Thanks,

Ramu.

entry.title: Oracle DB TCPS Connection issue - Integration Suite
entry.content:
I am trying to connect to an On-Prem Oracle 19c DB in Integration Suite. 
Cloud Connector connectivity is fine.
I tried the following:
 
1. JDBC URL provided (Old style Full Address format):
This URL was provided in the JDBC Material, Oracle driver was also installed
 pod1234.test-testing.com is a virtual host
 
jdbc:oracle:thin:@(DESCRIPTION=(ADDRESS=(PROTOCOL=tcps)(HOST=pod1234.test-testing.com)(PORT=1539))(CONNECT_DATA=(SERVICE_NAME=TEST_ABC3)))
 
Outcome: fails with error com.sap.it.rt.adapter.jdbc.exceptions.JDBCException: Error while getting Datasource Service: ServiceReference is null
 
2. Tried EZCONNECT Style since I think Full Address format is not supported: 
a) jdbc:oracle:thin:@tcps:pod1234.test-testing.com:1539/TEST_ABC3?oracle.net.ssl_server_cert_dn="CN=pod1234a"
b) jdbc:oracle:thin:@tcps://pod1234.test-testing.com:1539/TEST_ABC3?oracle.net.ssl_server_cert_dn="CN=pod1234a"
 
Outcome: fails with the same error com.sap.it.rt.adapter.jdbc.exceptions.JDBCException: Error while getting Datasource Service: ServiceReference is null
 
The following URL worked fine: 
3. jdbc:oracle:thin:@pod1234.test-testing.com:1521/TEST_ABC3
but this is using the TCP protocol, and I want to connect using the TCPS protocol.
 
Please note that the cloud connector configuration is working fine; I don't think there is a problem here.
Additionally, I had also uploaded the certificate required for the TCPS connection SSL handshake in the Monitor > "Integration and APIs" > Keystore.
 
In the local system using DB Client DataGrip, I was able to connect using all the above URLs, but in Integration Suite, I am getting the error.
 
Please suggest what could be the issue.

entry.title: Exactly once in order (EOIO) for Group messages using Advanced Event Mesh
entry.content:
Hi there,

We have recently introduced SAP Advanced Event Mesh (AEM), which under the hood is ‘Solace PubSub+ Cloud’ in our Integration Landscape, and now I am seeking for the best way to implement ‘Exactly Once In Order’ Sequencing in our Integration.

We are on Version 10.9.1.114-0.

The Integration is going to do the following:

  1. Source System will Publish the Switching Job details (XML) to SAP AEM from WebLogic JMS Server.
  2. We have another SAP Integration Layer which then subscribes to the Solace Queue (or Topic), does Message Transformation, and then forwards that to External System.

The Challenge:

We want messages belonging to one Job (lets say Job A), should be delivered to External System in Sequence. Lets say Job A has Operation 1, 2 and 3. If for some reason Operation 2 fails to get delivered to External System, Job 3 should not go. However, Job B and it’s Operations can continue to go as usual.

So, we need FIFO for individual Job Operation, and not across all the Job Operations.

Analysis done so far:

  1. We can create One Exclusive Queue and One DMQ for the process, and Publish everything there. By doing it all the Jobs and their Operations will follow Sequence by Default. In All the Success scenarios, this should be okay (I guess). But in case of failure, the message will move to DMQ and the Subsequent message for one Job will not know and ‘might’ get processed.
  2. I went through Partition Queues, but that doesn’t seem to fit the purpose, as there the messages (Job Operations in our case) of one Job can go to any Partition
  3. Non-Durable (Temporary) Queues: What I have understood so far, we can request the Publisher (Client) to create Queues Dynamically and all the messages (Operation) of one Job should be sent to ONE Queue. As soon as those messages are consumed by our SAP Integration and forwarded to Externally System, these Queues get deleted - I am not 100% sure if this is how this will behave. In case of failures, I would expect the Queue remains intact and messages belonging to that Queue will pile up, without impacting the other Jobs.
  4. We can introduce a persistence layer to store the failed messages. Every message consumed from the queue will first be checked (using some checksum) to see if any message for that Operation has failed in the past and if yes, the push the message to DMQ or else continue normal. I will then have to keep DMQ and this persistence layer in sync. Or just use this persistence and not DMQ.

Following things are Mandatory:

  1. Guaranteed Delivery of the messages to External System
  2. Order of Operations belonging to same Job should be maintained
  3. Order for Different Jobs is not Mandatory, and those can be parallelly processed.

Any help on this would be really appreciated.

-Anuj

entry.title: Instance creation for apiportal-apiaccess plan in btp trial fails
entry.content:
Hi , 

I am trying to create instance for my tria account, but it fails with below error. I was able to create the intance in 2024, but now It is not working anymore. Am I missing anything? I have subscribed to Integration suite and able to create api , public and view in the dev portal. 

https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/api-access-plan-for-api-portal


Creation 

Error 

Couldn't create an instance of service 'API Management, API portal' with plan 'apiportal-apiaccess'.
 

 

 


Deletion

Couldn't delete service instance 'api-trial'.
Instance ID: b5266bf9-d15d-4caf-8d46-cbd99734d134

Service instance api-trial: Service broker error: Service broker apimanagement-apiportal-trial failed with: Forbidden

Regards,

Muni

entry.title: How to configure SAP IAS as a proxy IDP for Principal Propagation to SuccessFactors via APIM
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently working on integrating an external system to our SAP SuccessFactors , enabling Principal Propagation using SAP API Management (APIM).
In our case , SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS) is acting as a proxy to our actual Identity Provider (IDP).

While implementing the workaround described in SAP KBA 3462930 (OAuthSAMLBearer Token Exchange Bundle), we need to configure the following parameters in SFSFContainer-fortemplate \APIProxy\FileResource\config.js - File:

 

  • sapapim.sf.samlissuer – Trusted Identity Provider (IDP) entity ID

  • sapapim.sf.keystorename – Name of the Keystore in SAP API Management

  • sapapim.sf.certname – Name of the Certificate stored inside that Keystore

We would appreciate guidance on the following points:

  1. When SAP IAS is acting as a proxy IDP, what value should be set for sapapim.sf.samlissuer? Should it be the entity ID of IAS itself, or the underlying corporate IDP?

  2. How to generate the certificate in SAP IAS and upload it into SAP API Management’s keystore for Principal Propagation.

  3. Is there an official guide or best practices for setting up SAP IAS as a proxy for Principal Propagation through SAP API Management towards SuccessFactors?

Any advice, step-by-step guidance, or reference links would be highly appreciated.

Thank you very much for your help!

entry.title: Calling an OData V4 custom action from within an iFlow.
entry.content:
Hi,

I've built a custom action in backend S4 using RAP. Its purpose is to sync a Z-table in S4 with data read from an on-prem SQL Server. It works perfectly from Postman when I call it via a POST to https://xxxHostnamexxxx:44300/sap/opu/odata4/sap/zui_i_cv_person_api_o4/srvd_a2x/sap/zui_i_cv_person/0001/people/SAP__self.upsert_many .

I've tried incorporating it into an iFlow using the OData adaptor. This works fine for standard operations, like say using GET to return the top 10 records for the people entity. This is with Address: http://xxxCloudConnectorVirtualhostxxxx:44300/sap/opu/odata4/sap/zui_i_cv_person_api_o4/srvd_a2x/sap/zui_i_cv_person/0001 and Resource Path: people.

From my Postman trial I know the URL to the custom action ends in people/SAP__self.upsert_many. I tried using this as the Resource Path, but I get the error "com.sap.it.rt.adapter.http.api.exception. HttpResponseException: An internal server error occured: Resource path not found people/SAP__self.upsert_many". Is it possible to invoke a custom action using the OData adaptor?

I then tried using the HTTP adaptor to do a POST to the explicit URL, but I have to manage the CSRF protection manually and I can't work out how to pass the CSRF token to the POST operation after having called a GET to obtain the token. In this case I get this error {"error":{"code":"/IWBEP/CM_V4H_RUN/043","message":"CSRF token is missing","@SAP__common.ExceptionCategory":"CSRF_Token_Missing"}}

This is my first time using ABAP RAP and first time with SAP BTP. Maybe passing data en masse via a custom action isn't the right approach. Any help would be appreciated. 

- Anthony.

entry.title: how to get participant handbook for codejam re: event based processes
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm currently following a codejam blog regarding event-based processes for sap build. here is the link of my current lesson: 3 - Explore SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh | SAP Tutorials

one of the prerequisite is a "participants handbook" which contains the system and credentials needed to follow the blog.

where can i get this? thanks

entry.title: Understanding Cloud Credits and API Call Charges in SAP BTP
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

We are in the process of implementing SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud along with SAP BTP (including Integration Suite and Cloud ALM). Recently, we were surprised by API call charges between Cloud ALM and Jira (a non-SAP system).

To avoid further unexpected costs and better plan our architecture, we’re trying to understand:

  •  How do SAP cloud credits work in real-world projects?
    When are they activated (start/end), what services do they typically cover, and how should we plan for usage across Integration Suite, Cloud ALM, etc.?
  •  How are API calls charged when integrating SAP Public Cloud with non-SAP systems, such as an external Order Entry System or PIM (Product Information Management)?

Any references, documentation, or guidance based on your experiences would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you in advance,

Prayash

entry.title: How to send Excel file as an attachment in body in API?
entry.content:
How to send Excel file as an attachment in body in API? 

API API Management SAP S/4HANA Cloud for credit integration (Foundry) ABAP Connectivity SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Implementing Event-Driven Architecture with Canonical Data Model in SAP CPI
entry.content:
Event-Driven Architecture (EDA):
Event-Driven Architecture is a design pattern in which system components communicate by producing and consuming events. An event represents a change of state (e.g., a new sales order, product update). In EDA, producers send events without knowing who consumes them, enabling loose coupling between systems.

Key benefits:

  • Decoupling

  • Real-time data flow

  • Scalability and flexibility

    Canonical Data Model (CDM):
    A Canonical Data Model is a standardized format for data exchange between systems. Instead of each system mapping data differently, a single unified model is used across all integrations.

    The Integration Flow implementation looks like this:



    Step 1: Prepare Canonical Data

  • Prepare your canonical XML/JSON payload. Here’s a sample XML code:

<Root>
	<Products>
		<Product>
			<Price>2.5</Price>
			<Rating>4</Rating>
			<DiscontinuedDate/>
			<ID>0</ID>
			<ReleaseDate>1992-01-01T00:00:00.000</ReleaseDate>
		</Product>
	</Products>
	<Categories>
		<Category>
			<ID>0</ID>
			<Name>Food</Name>
		</Category>
	</Categories>
	<Suppliers>
		<Supplier>
			<Address>
				<State>WA</State>
				<ZipCode>98074</ZipCode>
				<Street>NE 228th</Street>
				<Country>USA</Country>
				<City>Sammamish</City>
			</Address>
			<Concurrency>0</Concurrency>
			<ID>0</ID>
			<Name>Exotic Liquids</Name>
		</Supplier>
	</Suppliers>
	<Employee>
		<Name>Hirva</Name>
		<ID>4</ID>
	</Employee>
	<Book>
		<Book_ID>101</Book_ID>
		<Book_Title>Effective Java</Book_Title>
		<Book_Author>Joshua Bloch</Book_Author>
		<Book_Description>A comprehensive guide to best practices in Java programming.</Book_Description>
	</Book>
	<Customers>
		<Customer>
			<Number>1</Number>
			<FirstName>Fred</FirstName>
			<LastName>Landis</LastName>
			<Address>
				<Street>Oakstreet</Street>
				<City>Boston</City>
				<ZIP>23320</ZIP>
				<State>MA</State>
			</Address>
		</Customer>
	</Customers>
	<breakfast_menu>
		<food>
			<name>Belgian Waffles</name>
			<price>$5.95</price>
			<description>Two of our famous Belgian Waffles with plenty of real maple syrup</description>
			<calories>650</calories>
		</food>
	</breakfast_menu>
</Root>

Step 2: Send Data from Postman

  • Method: POST

  • URL: Your SAP CPI iFlow endpoint (e.g., https://<tenant>.iflmap.sap.hana.ondemand.com/http/canonicalEvent)

  • Header: Content-Type: application/xml

    Body: Paste the sample canonical message.

    Step 3: Build SAP CPI iFlow

    Component Purpose

    HTTPS Sender AdapterReceives HTTP POST from Postman
    Content Modifier (optional)Logs or adds initial data
    Router (Content-Based)Routes based on Type (SalesOrder, Product)
    JMS Receiver AdapterSends message to the target JMS queue (SalesOrderQueue, ProductQueue)
    parallel multicastAllows you to send the same message to multiple routes for parallel processing

    Router Conditions:

    If using XML XPath:

    • Condition 1 (Sales Order):
      //CanonicalMessage/Type = 'SalesOrder'

    • Condition 2 (Product):
      //CanonicalMessage/Type = 'Product'

    Step 4: Configure JMS Queues

    1.In SAP CPI Cockpit:

    • Go to JMS Resources

    • Create Queues:

      • SalesOrderQueue

      • ProductQueue

    2.In CPI iFlow:

    • Add JMS Receiver Adapter

    • Point each route to its respective queue.

    Step 5: Deploy & Test

    1.Deploy the iFlow.
    2.Post Data using Postman.
    3.Check Monitoring:

    • In SAP CPI Message Monitor, confirm successful routing.

    • In JMS Queue Monitor, confirm that the message lands in the correct queue.

    Error Handling: Sending Email on Exception


    Steps to Implement Email Notification in Error Handling:

    1. Add Exception Subprocess:
    In your main iFlow:

    • Drag and drop an Exception Subprocess (gray dotted box around your main flow).

    • This subprocess will automatically catch any unhandled exceptions in the main flow

    2.Add Error Handling Components:

    Component Purpose

    Start Event (Escalation)Starts the exception subprocess
    Content ModifierPrepares email content (subject, body, etc.)
    Mail Receiver AdapterSends the actual email


    3.Configure the Content Modifier (Prepare Email):

    Exchange Property:

    • EmailSubject: Error in CPI iFlow: Canonical Event Router

    • EmailBody: {As like you want}

      4. Configure Mail Receiver Adapter:

    • Protocol: Mail

    • SMTP Server: Your mail server (e.g., smtp.office365.com)

    • Authentication: Yes (configure in Security Materials)

    • To: e.g., support@yourcompany.com

    • Subject: ${property.EmailSubject}

    • Body: ${property.EmailBody}

    Make sure your CPI tenant has Mail (SMTP) connectivity set up.

    5. Deploy & Test:

    • Force an error: You can test by deliberately sending invalid data from Postman.

    • Check:

      • CPI Monitoring (Exception Subprocess should trigger)

      • Email inbox (verify error notification received)

entry.title: Guide to Generate PGP Public Key & Secret key for SAP CPI
entry.content:
To ensure compatibility with SAP CPI, here's how to generate PGP keys without using the AEAD algorithm.

1) Generate Public and Secret Keys

Step 1: Verify GnuPG installation.

  • Run “gpg –version”.

 

  • If not installed:
    • Windows: Download and install Gpg4win.
    • Linux: sudo apt-get update

sudo apt-get install gnupg

    • Mac: brew install gnupg

Step 2: Generate a PGP key pair.

  • Run: “gpg --full-generate-key”

When prompted, provide:

 

 

  • Key type: Choose option 1 (RSA and RSA).
  • Key size: 2048.
  • Validity period: For example, 2y (years), 9m (months), or 3w (weeks).
  • User ID details: Enter a unique name and email recognized by SAP CPI.
  • Passphrase: Create and securely store it (needed later).

 

 

Step 3: Export the public key.

“gpg --armor --export your.email@example.com > public_key.asc”

(For instance: gpg --armor --export sapcpi@gmail.com > public_key.asc)

 

  • Save the resulting file locally.

 

Step 4: Export the private (secret) key.

“gpg --armor --export-secret-keys your.email@example.com > private_key.asc”

(For instance: gpg --armor --export-secret-keys sapcpi@gmail.com > private_key.asc)

  • Enter your passphrase when prompted. The private key will be saved locally.

 

Step 5: Check if the generated key has AEAD_ALGORITHM.

 

  • If yes, run: “setpref AES256 AES192 AES128 3DES SHA512 SHA384 SHA256 SHA224 SHA1 ZLIB BZIP2 ZIP Uncompressed”

 

  • Otherwise, you can skip this step.

Step 6: (If needed) Repeat Step 3 and Step 4 to save updated public and private keys.

2) Configure the Generated Keys in SAP CPI

  • Log in to SAP CPI and navigate to Monitor => PGP Keys.

 

  • To encrypt messages, deploy the public key under "Manage PGP Keys".

 

 

Click on Add.

 

 

  • To decrypt messages, deploy the secret key and provide the passphrase in "Manage PGP Keys".

 

 

Click on Add,

 

 

  • Finally, create or update your interface to include the required encryption or decryption steps.

entry.title: Difference between Basic Authentication with clientid and client and OAuth with Client Credentials
entry.content:
Procedures for implementing the two approaches respectively can be seen here:
https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/basic-authentication-with-clientid-and-clientsecret-for-integration-flow-processing
https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/oauth-with-client-credentials-grant-for-integration-flow-processing

I understand the idea behind each option. However, I don't see how the implementations are different. It seems like service keys generated are the same, and then the adapter configurations in integration flows are also the same. If so, then what is the thing that makes a client be authorized with clientId/clientSecret vs a token?

Thanks

entry.title: SAP CPI is not picking few files from Sender SFTP AL11.
entry.content: Daily around 40 files will be placed in AL11 in one of the directory and initially due to some issue with 9 files the files went to processing mode in SAP CPI for 24 hrs and they we're discarded. Since then those file names are not being picked up by CPI, we tried to change the file name they we're successful but I need the exact file names to be processed from the same directory (client requirement) and post processing they should be placed in archive folder. Is there any solution to it, Receiver is also SFTP and we're reading the file name from property and on receiver side the filename is called through property so I cannot append timestamp. client requirement is the filename should be same daily so that they should overwrite the existing file daily. Below is the SFTP detail for reference.

entry.title: Accessing SAP Graph OData V4 through SAP Cloud Integration (CPI)
entry.content:
Introduction

This blog explains how to access an SAP Graph OData V4 URL using SAP Cloud Integration (CPI). We will configure the connection, use OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials, and query data using OData parameters.

What is SAP Graph?

SAP Graph is a unified API that provides a simplified, single interface to access data across various SAP systems (like SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors, SAP Sales Cloud, etc.). It helps developers interact with complex SAP systems using a consistent API structure, making integration and data access easier.

Prerequisites

  • Access to SAP BTP with an SAP Graph instance.

  • OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials for SAP Graph.

  • SAP CPI with design privileges.

Step 1: Create OAuth 2.0 Credentials

  1. In SAP BTP Cockpit, navigate to your SAP Graph instance.

  2. Go to Security and create OAuth 2.0 client credentials.

  3. Note the Client ID and Client Secret.

  4. Save the credentials in Security Material of CPI as a new OAuth2 Client Credentials artifact (e.g., Credential Name: GraphOAuthCredentials).

Step 2: Get the SAP Graph URL

  1. In SAP Integration Suite, navigate to the Design section.

  2. Inside Business Data Graph, you can find the OData V4 URL for your SAP Graph instance.

  3. Copy the OData V4 URL (e.g., https://<your-graph-url>/graph/api/graphdemo).

Step 3: Configure OData Connection in CPI

  1. Log in to SAP CPI, go to Design, and create an Integration Flow.

  2. Add a Start Timer and a Request Reply step.

    3. Set connection details:

  • Address: https://<your-graph-url>/graph/api/graphdemo/sap.s4

  • Authentication: OAuth2 Client Credentials

  • Credential Name: GraphOAuthCredentials

 

Step 3: Set the Resource Path

 

  • Resource Path: A_SalesOrder

  • Query: $select=SalesOrder,SoldToParty&$filter=SalesOrder eq '1'&$expand=_SoldToParty

Step 4: Deploy and Test

  1. Deploy the integration flow.

  2. Monitor message processing to verify the response.

Conclusion

You have successfully configured SAP CPI to access SAP Graph OData V4 using OAuth 2.0 authentication.

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite for DEV and PROD
entry.content:
Hello,

we want to setup SAP Integration Suite to implement iFlows. 
So we need a DEV system to design the iFlows and a PROD system for the released iFlows.
What ist the best way to setup this scenario? 
Do we need two subaccounts in BTP Cockpit? One for DEV and one for PROD or is it possible to separate the two areas within the Intergration Suite, or can we use two different space in Cloud Foundry Environment?

Thanks for your proposals.

entry.title: Direct API call from SAP S/4 HANA public cloud to third party cloud system without Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

We are working on requirement to integrate SAP and non-SAP system. We have SAP S/4 HANA public cloud edition through which we need to do API call (GET/POST), provided by third party cloud system. I understand we can achieve same with SAP 'Integration suite service' (hosted on SAP BTP account) but in our case it is not an option to do so.

We are looking to do Direct API call ( like how can we do on ECC or S/4 on premise system, by using SM59 HTTP connection and using cl_http* class) .

But can we achieve same with SAP S/4 HANA public cloud edition without any integration (middleware) system?

It would be very helpful if someone has any insights regarding this.

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: how to set CPI Log Configuration to trace on for a single Iflow for only one scheduled run only
entry.content: I have a scheduled run at 6 am for a single interface that i want to have log configuration set to Trace, it will automatically default to info by the SAP Internal jobs, how do i enable this from code/configuration for ONLY this run.

entry.title: Would it be possible for you to help me with the following question:- the Baltimore Root
entry.content:
Good afternoon,
Would it be possible for you to help me with the following question:
- the Baltimore CyberTrust Root Certificate that I have in the Integration Suite will expire, but according to SAP note 3565626 it will no longer be renewed.
How then to proceed in the case of the Integration Suite?
If I already have DigiCert Global Root CA and DigiCert Global Root G2 Certificates, do I need to take any extra action?
I would appreciate a certain urgency
Thank you very much in advance
